findings.summary: Connector-only adapter board correctly parsed: 6 connectors, 3 named nets, 38 no-connects

correct.description: The KiCad 5 legacy schematic is a breakout/adapter board for an SSD1306 OLED, MAXBOTIX MB1010 distance sensor, and Adafruit Metro Mini MCU. The analyzer correctly identifies all 6 connectors (4x PPPC071LFBN-RC and 2x PPPC141LFBN-RC), the +5V and GND power rails, and the 38 no-connect markers on unused connector pins. The net count of 3 (+5V, GND, LOGO) accurately reflects the schematic — the PCB has 46 nets because each connector pin gets an auto-named net, which is expected for a passthrough adapter board.

findings.summary: aligned=True despite F.SilkS extending significantly beyond board outline

incorrect.description: The gerber alignment check reports aligned=True with no issues, but the F.SilkS layer extents are 62.752mm x 114.348mm while the Edge.Cuts outline is only 55.5mm x 95.5mm. The front silkscreen is ~7mm taller and ~19mm wider than the board outline. This is a real alignment/overflow issue that should be flagged — silkscreen graphics extend outside the PCB boundary. The silkscreen label text ('ADAFRUIT METRO MINI', 'MAXBOTIX MB1010', 'SSD1306 OLED LCD') appears to be the cause. The analyzer should warn when a silkscreen layer bbox significantly exceeds the Edge.Cuts bbox.
